name: Ginger Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 1 1/2 hours
Chilling time: 12 hours or more
Cooking time: 7 minutes
1. Combine sugar, syrup, water, ginger, cinnamon, and cloves in a large saucepan. Cook and stir over medium heat until the mixture boils and sugar dissolves. Remove from heat. Add butter. Stir until butter is melted and mixture is no longer hot.
2. Mix flour and baking soda. Gradually add this to the butter mixture and stir until thoroughly blended. The dough will be soft. Place in an airtight container and refrigerate overnight or at least 12 hours, up to 1 week.
3. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Remove about one-sixth of the dough and knead it until slightly softened. Roll directly onto ungreased cookie sheets until about 1/4-inch thick. Use a cookie cutter to stamp shapes in the dough, leaving a 1-inch margin between each cookie. Remove excess dough by lifting and peeling. Save scraps for re-rolling.
4. Bake until golden brown, about 7 minutes. Let cookies cool slightly before removing from sheets. Cool on wire racks. If desired, paint cooled cookies with food coloring watered down using a small brush. Store in airtight containers.
Note: After the 12-hour resting period, dough can be hand-molded like clay—rolled, pinched, poked, and pressed—and will retain its shape while expanding slightly during baking. Thin cookies become brown quickly, larger or thicker shapes need longer baking time. Cut into desired shapes but keep thickness uniform. Sugar mixture can also be cooked in a microwave oven.
